mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output that immediately pressures equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of observed instances during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0 percent, market participants anticipate aggressive central bank accommodation that fuels risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat when FEDFUNDS drops below 2.0 percent, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand that immediately drags down cyclical equity sectors, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 58 percent of periods where HOUST drops below 1100.0 units.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion that underpins corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an out-of-sample hit rate below 50 percent across rolling windows.
